methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Sunday Robotics is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Tony Zhao and Cheng Chi that has raised $200M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a sector universe of 12 robotics and manufacturing peers, comprising 1 public company and 8 private firms including 1X Technologies, Foundation Robotics, Divergent, Exotec, and Locus Robotics.

Stage-progression methodology is the applicable valuation approach here. Using total capital raised of $200M as a proxy for asset base and applying a standard Seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.5x yields an implied valuation of $500M. Peer-median triangulation was not feasible; while 12 same-sector peers exist in the robotics and manufacturing space, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation-tier comparables yielded fewer than three datapoints, making a reliable median unworkable. An all-sector median would risk material distortion.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $425M (low) to $575M (high), with a median of $500M. This single-method conclusion carries meaningful uncertainty given reliance on stage-progression mechanics rather than peer validation. Critical uncertainties include capital efficiency relative to stage peers, the composition and deployment stage of the $200M raised, and whether robotics sector multiples are stabilizing or subject to continued compression. Validation through comparable transaction data or refined peer clustering would strengthen conviction in this range.
